design.summary: M13 fills the body behind the born-with ObjKind::MemoryHome handle (minted at M12 spawn, slot0/gen1, rights READ|WRITE|RECALL) so every agent's reserved home becomes a real per-agent tiered, persistent, recallable substrate — a kernel guarantee reached ONLY through the single M11 dispatch chokepoint, rights-gated, namespaced memory:private/<agent>. Land it as a NEW safe module crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs (`#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`), wired beside `pub mod caps;` in lib.rs. The substrate = bounded T0 context registers + T1 state-rooted working graph + append-only bi-temporal T2 episodic journal (instant read-your-writes) + lexical (BM25) T3 semantic store with activation-ranked recall. Recall is the Zep 3-stage pipeline (candidate -> RRF/MMR rerank -> templated context) over the additive default score `w_a*BLA(d=0.5) + w_r*relevance + w_i*importance` (components min-max normalized, default w=1), with Finsts/exclude_recent breaking the return-same-result loop and copy-on-retrieve handing back an owned clone. ABI: keep M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 reserved for the privileged T4 procedural path (preserves the CoALA risk asymmetry); ADD M_MEM_WRITE=23 (gated WRITE) + M_MEM_READ=24 (gated READ) in the documented sparse band, reuse M_MEM_RECALL=19 (RECALL) and M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20 (CONSOLIDATE). ALL math is fixed-point integer (deterministic/replayable, no kernel FPU hazard, Kani-friendly, zero deps) over safe heap structures behind a BackingStore trait (RamStore now; durable virtio-blk deferred). ZERO new unsafe. Marker: `M13: memory OK`.
design.tier_design:
FOUR tiers, all safe-Rust heap structures in crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs, owned by one `pub struct MemSubstrate { t0, t1, t2, t3, finsts, clock: u64, quota: Quota, backing: Box<dyn BackingStore> }`. `clock` is a monotonic logical transaction counter (deterministic; M8 ticks optional) feeding bi-temporal stamps and BLA age. One MemSubstrate lives behind each agent's born-with MemoryHome Object == structurally the memory:private/<agent> namespace.

T0 CONTEXT REGISTERS (ACT-R buffers / MemGPT main context). STORES: a fixed, const-bounded array `registers: [Option<Register>; N_REG]`, N_REG=8 named typed slots (goal, retrieval, percept, tool_result, plan, scratch, +2 reserved). `struct Register { name_tok: u64, kind: RegKind, value: u64 }`. The prompt is materialized EXCLUSIVELY from T0 — there is no unbounded context blob. BOUNDS: each slot a hard token budget (~few hundred tokens); total T0 = the prompt budget; write is O(1) overwrite. EVICTION: writing a slot overwrites O(1); the displaced value is NOT lost (it was journaled into T2 on the cycle it appeared, optionally promoted into T1 if still state-reachable). This is where tb_recall's context-constructor output lands via copy-on-retrieve and where percepts/tool-results ingest (the survey's sensory tier folds in here).

T1 WORKING GRAPH (Soar WM). STORES: `struct WorkingGraph { nodes: Vec<WmNode>, root: u32 }`, `struct WmNode { id: u32, attr_tok: u64, val: u64, edges: Vec<u32>, support: Support }`, `enum Support { ISupport, OSupport }` (i-support = justifying thought, auto-retracted). BOUNDS: a node/edge quota (low hundreds of nodes). EVICTION: mark-and-sweep reachability from `root` — nodes unreachable from the state root are auto-GC'd (i-support retraction), a pure safe Vec walk. Lossless because T2 already holds the append-only record.

T2 EPISODIC JOURNAL (Soar EpMem / MemGPT recall storage). STORES the append-only, lossless, bi-temporal flight-recorder. Use a SEGMENTED arena `log: Vec<Box<[Episode; CHUNK]>>` (chunked, NOT one giant Vec) for stable addresses + O(1) amortized append with bounded worst-case latency, and so one segment == one future virtio-blk page. `struct Episode { id: u64, content_tok: u64, value: u64, t_created: u64, t_expired: u64, t_valid: u64, t_invalid: u64, provenance: Provenance, producing_task: u32 }`. RecordId = monotonically increasing index. Append = push; the instant push returns the record is visible to same-agent read/recall — that IS instant read-your-writes (no ingestion lag, the Zep counter-example). BOUNDS: token-quota'd via Quota (KeyKOS space-bank; write-amplification can exceed 20x so cap it). EVICTION: NEVER deleted — only tombstoned (set t_invalid) or score-decay demoted to T5 archival (M17). T2 is the lossless floor; index agent OUTPUTS too, not just inputs (Zep -17.7% single-session lesson).

T3 SEMANTIC STORE (ACT-R declarative / MemGPT archival), lexical-only at M13 (dense channel inert until M16). STORES: `struct SemanticStore { records: Vec<MemRecord>, lexical: BTreeMap<u64 /*token*/, Vec<Posting>>, doc_len: Vec<u32>, total_len: u64, num_docs: u32 }`, `struct Posting { doc: u32, tf: u32 }` (record-level inverted index = BM25+; df = postings.len(); avgdl = total_len/num_docs is O(1)). `MemRecord` carries the MEMORY-SPEC §2 scoring fields: `importance: u8 (1..=10)` (GA poignancy), `access: { count: u32, last_k_ts: [u64; 10] }` (O(1) BLA state, ACT-R/Petrov), `utility: { c_succ: u32, c_use: u32 }`, `links: Vec<Link { kind: LinkKind(Cites|Relates|Supersedes), to: u32 }>` (cites back to T2 source for hallucination audit), bi-temporal t_* stamps, provenance, and a visible freshness `epoch: u64` (derived tier staleness marker; T2 stays the instant-truth backstop). BOUNDS: <1KB/record; Quota-metered growth. EVICTION: lowest-BLA records demote T3->T5 (M17), tombstone not delete. Records stored as `Rc<MemRecord>` so recall hands back cheap immutable Rc snapshots (copy-on-retrieve).

EVICTION CHAIN (the live path): T0 displace (O(1), already in T2) -> T1 GC (unreachable swept, already in T2) -> T2 is the lossless floor (tombstone/demote only) -> T2->T3 is async CONSOLIDATION not eviction (M17 daemon distills journal into semantic records on an importance-accumulator trigger ~150, never on the critical path). "Eviction" never destroys — it moves data DOWN the hierarchy (MemOS MemCube migration).
design.recall_pipeline:
tb_recall = M_MEM_RECALL (method 19, gated RECALL) against the born-with memory_home -> the THREE-STAGE pipeline (Zep-shaped), NOT a monolithic search. All math fixed-point integer (SCALE=1000), tie-broken by RecordId so ordering is a total deterministic order (required for Finsts retrieve_next).

STAGE 0 (prep): tokenize the query token id(s); resolve the agent's ACL (capability handle -> memory:private/<agent>) and its Finsts exclude ring.

STAGE 1 (candidate generation — parallel channels, each emits a RANKED Vec<RecordId> truncated to depth Kc~50-200):
- Lexical/BM25+ channel: term-at-a-time over `lexical` postings, INTEGER scoring. Per (term,doc): lenNorm = (SCALE - B) + (B*|D|)/avgdl with B=750; num = f*(K1+SCALE) with K1=1200; denom = f*SCALE + (K1*lenNorm)/SCALE; tf_sat = (num*OUT)/denom + DELTA (BM25+ delta=SCALE); contrib += (idf_fixed*tf_sat)/OUT, all saturating_/checked_ u64. IDF computed LAZILY at query time once per distinct query term as the non-negative Lucene variant ln(1 + (N - df + 0.5)/(df + 0.5)) via a ~10-line fixed-point ln (integer log2 = 63 - x.leading_zeros() + tiny mantissa LUT, times ln2 scaled). Negative-IDF hazard (small per-agent corpus) avoided by this variant.
- Dense/cosine channel: define the trait NOW, return EMPTY at M13 (lights up when M16 fills MemRecord.embedding).
- Graph/BFS channel over links[]: optional, off by default.
- Apply the bitemporal asof filter (default asof_tx=now, any valid range) + ACL + Finsts EXCLUDE here, so invalid/excluded records never enter fusion.

STAGE 2 (fusion + diversify):
- RRF merges channel lists by RANK only (no cross-channel normalization): rrf(d) = sum_c w_c*RRF_SCALE/(k + rank_c(d)), k=60, RRF_SCALE~1e6, accumulated as u64 in a BTreeMap<RecordId,u64> -> fused `relevance`.
- Compose the EXACT additive default score on the fused candidates:
    score(i | q) = w_a*BLA*(i) + w_r*relevance(i) + w_i*importance(i)
  defaults w_a = w_r = w_i = 1 (GA-validated, tunable per namespace); EACH of the three components min-max normalized to fixed-point [0,SCALE] OVER THE CANDIDATE SET before summing. BLA(d=0.5) is the ONLY recency/frequency term — it REPLACES GA's 0.995^hours (encodes recency AND frequency in one scalar, better-validated). Fixed-point BLA, three fidelities matching the schema: n<=10 EXACT B = ln(sum_{j=1..n}(t_now-t_j)^-0.5); n>10 HYBRID (exact last-10 + approximated tail); O(1) fallback B ~= ln(2n) - 0.5*ln(L) (Petrov optimized-learning, L = age) using only {count, t_created}. importance(i) = MemRecord.importance/10. Spreading activation, partial matching, and noise OFF by default (ACT-R conservatism); when spreading is later opted-in use the fan-discounted S_ji = S_max - ln(fan_j) so hubs can't dominate.
- MMR diversifies the top-N by score: argmax_{d∉S}[ lambda*score(d) - (1-lambda)*max_{s∈S} Sim(d,s) ], lambda~700/1000. Sim = integer lexical Jaccard over token-id sets (|A∩B|*SCALE/|A∪B|) at M13 (no embeddings; upgrades to cosine at M16). Run on N~20, pick k~5 (bounded O(N*k)). Seed S with the Finsts set so recently-returned items push novelty.

STAGE 3 (context constructor): COPY-ON-RETRIEVE the MMR-ordered top-k — return an OWNED clone (Soar LTI->STI) into the T0 `retrieval` register, rendered through a template carrying bi-temporal validity ranges + provenance/cite tags. The canonical store (T2 journal, T3 index) mutates ONLY via explicit tb_mem_write/commit; in Rust the recall path is literally a clone of an Rc<MemRecord> snapshot — no aliasing into the store.

FINSTS / exclude_recent / retrieve_next (the 40-year ACT-R loop breaker): `struct Finsts { recent: [Option<(u64 id, u64 ts)>; F] }`, a bounded + time-limited per-agent VecDeque-style ring (ACT-R default 4 items / 3 s, count+span scaled to TABOS session length, an OPEN constant). exclude_recent=true == ACT-R :recently-retrieved nil (subtract the finst set from candidates); retrieve_next pushes the just-returned IDs into the ring and re-runs the pipeline -> advances instead of looping.

READ-PATH SIDE EFFECTS: on each successful recall update access.count++ and push t_now into the last_k=10 ring (relatime-style batched so the read path stays cheap) so BLA stays current; push returned IDs into the Finsts ring. Budget p95 < 200 ms (Mem0-proven); raw-episode replay over T2 is the always-addressable high-stakes escape hatch.
design.tb_mem_abi:
Pointer-free, over the EXISTING M11 descriptor SyscallArgs { method: u32, handle: Handle, args: [u64;4] } -> SysReturn { status, value: u64 } (ONE scalar out). Target handle is always the born-with memory_home cap; values/keys are INLINE SCALARS or object handles at M13 (real text buffers arrive with M14 channels; copy_to_user/copy_from_user are M14-unsafe, so M13 stays content-light/kernel-staged). Every call routes through the single caps::dispatch chokepoint, gated by required_right() BEFORE the body runs.

THE ABI GAP + RESOLUTION (the central decision, confirmed against caps.rs+lib.rs): the born-with home carries plain WRITE (lib.rs:1285 mh_rights = READ|WRITE|RECALL), but the only reserved write method M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 requires WRITE_PROCEDURAL (1<<7) — an ordinary tb_mem_write would be Denied. RESOLUTION: KEEP method 18 / WRITE_PROCEDURAL reserved for the T4 procedural write (lands M18) to preserve the CoALA risk asymmetry, and ADD two bodies in the documented sparse band (caps.rs header: "M13 adds method BODIES"; highest existing is M_BUDGET_DELEGATE=22):
  - `pub const M_MEM_WRITE: u32 = 23;` -> required_right = Rights::WRITE
  - `pub const M_MEM_READ:  u32 = 24;` -> required_right = Rights::READ
Reuse M_MEM_RECALL=19 (RECALL) and M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20 (CONSOLIDATE). Add 23/24 arms to required_right() and 19/20/23/24 arms to dispatch(). NO new rights bit, NO manifest change, NO handle/spawn-signature change — purely additive, M11 rights-mask invariant intact.

ENCODINGS (args = [u64;4]):
- tb_mem_write  (M_MEM_WRITE=23, WRITE): args[0]=op {0 ADD, 1 UPDATE, 2 DELETE->tombstone, 3 NOOP} (Mem0 four-op vocab); args[1]=key/lexical-token id (interned small-int word/tag from userspace; self-test uses synthetic ids — NO embeddings); args[2]=value scalar (or a Handle.raw() to richer content later); args[3]=packed { importance 1..=10 in low byte (GA poignancy), tier+provenance flags in high bytes }. Body: append a MemRecord to T2 (assign id, stamp bi-temporal times from clock, record provenance = caller principal/task, namespace memory:private/<agent>), index into T3 lexical inline (instant RYW), meter Quota. Returns SysReturn::ok(record_id). UPDATE/DELETE append a new bitemporal version + set the prior tx_to + a supersedes link (contradiction = invalidate, never in-place mutate).
- tb_mem_read   (M_MEM_READ=24, READ): args[0]=record_id. Returns ok(value) — the INSTANT read-your-writes path over the append-only T2 journal; no ranking, no LLM.
- tb_recall     (M_MEM_RECALL=19, RECALL): args[0]=query token id; args[1]=Finsts cursor / exclude_recent flag (0=fresh, else retrieve_next continuation); args[2]=k/max; args[3]=packed tier-mask + optional w_a/w_r/w_i (default all=1). Runs the 3-stage pipeline; COPY-ON-RETRIEVE mints an ObjKind::MemoryRecord (already reserved) working-copy into the caller's table and returns ok(record_handle.raw()); the agent then M_OBJECT_INSPECT/reads the scalar. Subsequent calls with the cursor advance past the just-returned (finsted) set.
- tb_mem_manage / consolidate (M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20, CONSOLIDATE): args[0]=manage op {set t_invalid tombstone, add typed link, demote tier, pin into a T0 register}; args[1..]=operands. Returns ok(count). M13 ships a minimal SYNCHRONOUS consolidate to prove the gated path; the async kswapd-style daemon is M17.

Because SysReturn carries a single u64, recall/read return an id-or-handle, never a buffer — fully pointer-free, M11-faithful, forward-compatible with the M14 channel/M15 block buffer payload path. NOTE: lib.rs agent_syscall_current currently passes args [a0,a1,0,0] (a2/a3 hardcoded 0) — extend it to plumb a2/a3 so the full write/recall encoding reaches dispatch (the kernel-side self-test that drives caps::dispatch directly already gets the full [u64;4] and does not depend on this).
design.substrate_module:
NEW FILE crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs, file-top inner attribute `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (mirrors caps.rs). Wire it in crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs at ~L41 beside `pub mod caps;`: add `pub mod mem;` (pub so the kernel self-test can name tb_hal::mem::* types). The crate is already #![no_std] with `extern crate alloc;` online crate-wide (lib.rs:38), so mem.rs uses alloc::{vec::Vec, boxed::Box, collections::BTreeMap, rc::Rc, string::String} freely — pure SAFE heap structures + indices. caps.rs gains `use core::cell::RefCell;` + `use crate::mem;` to hold the payload; both files stay forbid(unsafe). The crate's existing #![deny(missing_docs)] is crate-wide, so EVERY pub item in mem.rs (and the new caps.rs consts/fn) needs a doc comment or the build fails. NO Cargo.toml change: all math is fixed-point integer (no libm/float dep) — tb-hal keeps depending only on tb-boot.

HOW THE SUBSTRATE ATTACHES TO MemoryHome (zero-unsafe; keeps the M11 chokepoint the only door): caps::Object is identity-only today (`pub struct Object { pub kind: ObjKind }`). Give it an optional interior-mutable payload: `pub struct Object { pub kind: ObjKind, pub mem: Option<RefCell<crate::mem::MemSubstrate>> }`. Keep current mint()/dup/narrow/transfer constructing `Object { kind, mem: None }`. Add `HandleTable::mint_memory_home(rights) -> Option<Handle>` that builds `Object { kind: MemoryHome, mem: Some(RefCell::new(MemSubstrate::new(Box::new(RamStore::default())))) }`. In agent_spawn (lib.rs:1285) swap the born-with line `table.mint(ObjKind::MemoryHome, mh_rights)` for `table.mint_memory_home(mh_rights)`. Each agent thus gets its OWN MemSubstrate behind its OWN MemoryHome Object = the memory:private/<agent> namespace. Rc+RefCell is the right pair: dispatch holds &mut HandleTable and reaches the object via shared Rc<Object>; RefCell yields the &mut MemSubstrate for write/recall, and the single-core/interrupts-masked discipline AGENTS/ctx_switch already rely on guarantees no reentrant borrow. (Rejected alternative: a side registry keyed by principal — breaks "reached ONLY through the handle" and changes the dispatch signature.) COPY-ON-RETRIEVE borrow rule: in the dispatch arm, CLONE the Rc<Object> out and DROP the slot borrow BEFORE table.alloc/mint of the MemoryRecord, then borrow_mut the substrate through the cloned Rc — so the table &mut never aliases the in-flight RefCell borrow.

BackingStore seam (durable virtio-blk slots in later WITHOUT touching tier logic): `pub trait BackingStore { fn append(&mut self, region: Region, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<u64, MemErr>; fn read_at(&self, region: Region, off: u64, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, MemErr>; fn flush(&mut self) -> Result<(), MemErr>; fn epoch(&self) -> u64; }` with `enum Region { Episodic, Semantic, Working }`, `enum MemErr { Full, NotFound, Io }`. M13 default `pub struct RamStore { ep: Vec<u8>, sem: Vec<u8>, wm: Vec<u8>, epoch: u64 }` over the M5/M7 kernel heap (append extends the per-region Vec + bumps epoch; flush is a no-op). MemSubstrate holds `backing: Box<dyn BackingStore>` so a future VirtioBlkStore (durable; the ONLY unsafe, isolated in arch) drops in unchanged; epoch() is the T3 freshness marker. A Quota (KeyKOS space-bank) meters token write-amplification so one agent can't OOM the heap; HandleTable cap is 16, so copy-on-retrieve MemoryRecord mints can hit ObjFull — return the closed status, never panic.
design.selftest:
SLOT: a new `{ ... }` block in kernel/src/main.rs placed AFTER `tb_hal::serial_write_str("M12: agent OK\n");` and BEFORE the final `tb_hal::halt()`. Mirror M11/M12 style: a local `fn m13_fail(why: &str) -> ! { serial_write_str("M13: FAIL "); serial_write_str(why); serial_write_byte(b'\n'); halt() }` so the runner's grep fails loudly, ending with `tb_hal::serial_write_str("M13: memory OK\n");`. The timer was already timer_disarm()'d before the M12 verdict, so M13 runs SINGLE-THREADED with interrupts masked — exactly the discipline the MemSubstrate RefCell relies on. NO userspace embeddings: all keys/queries are synthetic interned token ids chosen in the test.

WITNESS A (self-contained kernel-side proof, primary — like the M11 algebra block; drives caps::dispatch directly so the full [u64;4] encoding is exercised):
  Build a local `caps::HandleTable::with_capacity(16)`; let home = mint_memory_home(READ|WRITE|RECALL|CONSOLIDATE) (a home with ALL four rights for the success paths).
  1. WRITE: dispatch(M_MEM_WRITE, home, [op=ADD, key=TOK_K, value=V0, packed(importance=9)]) -> assert status==Ok; let id0 = ret.value. Add 2-3 more records with varied tokens/importance/recency (e.g. id1 key=TOK_K importance=3 older; id2 key=TOK_J importance=7).
  2. READ-YOUR-WRITES (instant): dispatch(M_MEM_READ, home, [id0]) -> assert status==Ok && value==V0, immediately after the write (proves instant RYW over append-only T2; no ingestion lag).
  3. RECALL (activation-ranked): dispatch(M_MEM_RECALL, home, [query=TOK_K, cursor=0, k=1, weights=default]) -> assert status==Ok; let h = ret.value. Assert h resolves Ok with M_OBJECT_INSPECT to ObjKind::MemoryRecord (copy-on-retrieve minted a working copy). Assert the returned record is id0 (highest additive score = high importance 9 + recent, beats id1). Then RECALL again with the Finsts cursor / exclude_recent=1 -> assert it returns the NEXT candidate (id1), NOT id0 again (proves the return-same-result breaker advances).
  4. CONSOLIDATE (gated SUCCESS): dispatch(M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE, home, [op=tombstone, id1]) -> assert status==Ok; then dispatch(M_MEM_READ, home, [id1]) and assert the record's t_invalid is now set (or a returned dedup count > 0) — proving the privileged maintenance op ran because this home HOLDS CONSOLIDATE.
  5. RIGHTS-DENIED paths (fall straight out of the existing required_right gate, no special-casing):
     - re-mint home_ro = mint_memory_home(READ|RECALL) (no WRITE) -> dispatch(M_MEM_WRITE, home_ro, ...) -> assert status==Denied.
     - dispatch(M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE, home_ro, ...) -> assert status==Denied (no CONSOLIDATE right).
     - sanity: dispatch with a method whose right the home lacks stays Denied; an unknown method stays BadMethod (closed set unchanged).

WITNESS B (through-the-born-with-home, the kernel-guarantee witness; the existing manifests already line it up): MANIFEST_A home = READ|WRITE|RECALL (write OK, recall OK, consolidate DENIED — no CONSOLIDATE bit), MANIFEST_B home = READ|RECALL (write DENIED — clean denied path, zero new setup). Add a tb-hal facade (AGENTS is private) e.g. `pub fn agent_mem_dispatch(task, method, a0,a1,a2,a3) -> Option<(u32,u64)>` that runs caps::dispatch against the agent's stored memory_home handle. Assert: agent A write -> RYW read -> recall all Ok on A's ACTUAL born-with home; agent A consolidate -> Denied; agent B write -> Denied. This proves the substrate is a real per-agent guarantee behind the handle, not a local toy. (Requires extending agent_syscall_current / adding the facade to plumb a2/a3.)

VERDICT: ALL of {RYW value match, recall picks the ranked record, Finsts advances to the next id, consolidate Ok on the privileged home, both denied paths Denied} must hold together before printing `M13: memory OK`.
design.new_unsafe: ZERO new unsafe — confirmed and structural, not aspirational. (1) The substrate is a NEW module crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs with file-top `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, built entirely from safe alloc heap structures (Vec, Box, BTreeMap, BinaryHeap/insertion-Vec, Rc, VecDeque-style ring, fixed arrays) + indices. (2) caps.rs stays `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`: the Object payload uses core::cell::RefCell (safe interior mutability, no_std-OK) for the &mut MemSubstrate, sound because M13 dispatch runs single-core with interrupts masked (the same discipline AGENTS/ctx_switch already assume — no reentrant borrow). (3) ALL ranking math is FIXED-POINT INTEGER (BM25+, RRF, MMR/Jaccard, BLA via a ~10-line integer-log2 + mantissa-LUT ln) — NO floats, so no libm dependency and, critically, no kernel FPU hazard (kernels do not save SSE/x87 state on syscall entry; integer-only sidesteps it) and the math is bit-exact/replayable and Kani-verifiable. (4) The new ABI is purely additive method BODIES + two consts (M_MEM_WRITE=23/WRITE, M_MEM_READ=24/READ) in the documented sparse band; no new Rights bit, no new ObjKind, no handle-layout/dispatch-signature change. The ONLY unsafe in tb-hal remains the pre-existing per-arch register-lift shim (arch::*::user) and the AGENTS UnsafeCell cell — neither touched by M13. Durable backing (virtio-blk, the eventual sole new unsafe) is deferred behind the BackingStore trait, isolated to arch when it lands at M16+. tb-hal stays the sole unsafe-bearing crate; mem.rs and caps.rs are 100% safe Rust.
risks: WRITE vs WRITE_PROCEDURAL mismatch (the central decision): reserved M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 requires WRITE_PROCEDURAL but the born-with home (lib.rs:1285) carries only plain WRITE, so a T2/T3 write via method 18 would be Denied. RESOLVE by adding M_MEM_WRITE=23(WRITE)+M_MEM_READ=24(READ) and KEEPING 18/WRITE_PROCEDURAL for the privileged T4 procedural write at M18 (preserves the CoALA risk asymmetry). Do NOT add WRITE_PROCEDURAL to mh_rights (would violate 'no manifest change') and do NOT silently downgrade method 18's right (erases the asymmetry).

'No ABI change' status (ROADMAP-V2 L380) vs needing write/read/manage+recall = 4 verbs over 3 reserved numbers. Reconcile per the caps.rs header ('M13 adds method BODIES'): adding 23/24 in the explicitly-sparse band grows the closed method set without breaking the M11 rights-mask invariant — but flag it as a deliberate, reviewed choice, not a surprise.

CONSOLIDATE-right scope: neither born-with home nor MANIFEST_A/B grants CONSOLIDATE (1<<9), so the self-test's consolidate-SUCCESS path must use a locally-minted home with CONSOLIDATE; the born-with homes correctly supply the consolidate-DENIED path. Don't accidentally add CONSOLIDATE to the spawn defaults.

Object-payload mutation soundness: Object is shared behind Rc, so the MemSubstrate payload needs RefCell interior mutability; a reentrant borrow_mut panics. Safe ONLY because M13 dispatch runs single-core with interrupts masked. Copy-on-retrieve mints a MemoryRecord into the table (table &mut) WHILE reading the substrate — MUST clone the Rc<Object> out and drop the slot/RefCell borrow before table.alloc to avoid an aliasing/borrow conflict.

no_std math: core has no ln/powf and floats are a kernel FPU hazard (no SSE/x87 save on syscall entry) + non-deterministic across arch. Implement BLA(d=0.5), BM25+ IDF ln, and min-max normalization in FIXED-POINT INTEGER (integer-log2 + mantissa LUT); zero deps, deterministic/replayable, Kani-friendly. Do NOT reach for std or pull libm unless integer math proves insufficient.

#![deny(missing_docs)] is crate-wide: EVERY pub item in mem.rs (MemSubstrate, tiers, BackingStore, RamStore, MemRecord, the new caps.rs consts M_MEM_WRITE/M_MEM_READ and mint_memory_home) needs a doc comment or the build fails.

Heap pressure / unbounded growth: T2 is append-only and T3 carries a BTreeMap inverted index; without a token-quota (KeyKOS space-bank) a runaway writer can exhaust the M5/M7 heap. Bound T0 (const N_REG=8), T1 (node quota), Finsts (const F), and meter T2/T3 via Quota; HandleTable cap is 16, so copy-on-retrieve MemoryRecord mints can hit ObjFull — return the closed status, never panic.

Durability scope is RAM-only at M13 (BackingStore = RamStore); a crash loses memory — the accepted ROADMAP decision (durable virtio-blk deferred to M16+). The BackingStore trait + epoch() freshness marker MUST be in place now so durable backing slots in without touching tier logic.

Arg plumbing: lib.rs agent_syscall_current passes args [a0,a1,0,0] — the full [u64;4] write/recall encoding (op,key,value,packed-importance / query,cursor,k,weights) needs a2/a3 plumbed through (or a dedicated agent_mem_dispatch facade), else high args are silently zeroed for the through-the-agent path. The kernel-side caps::dispatch self-test already gets the full [u64;4] and does not depend on this.

Negative-IDF in a small per-agent corpus: classic BM25 IDF goes negative for terms in >half the records (common when an agent has few records). Use the non-negative Lucene/BM25+ variant ln(1 + (N-df+0.5)/(df+0.5)); break score ties by RecordId so recall is a total deterministic order (required for Finsts retrieve_next).

recon.memory_home_wiring:
HOW THE SUBSTRATE ATTACHES TO MemoryHome — three observed facts:

1. At spawn (crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs `agent_spawn`, ~L1269), the born-with home is minted FIRST and deterministically: `let memory_home = table.mint(ObjKind::MemoryHome, mh_rights)` where `mh_rights = READ|WRITE|RECALL` -> generation 1, slot 0, raw handle 0x0000_0001_0000_0000. It is delivered to the user-entry register file (`task_stack_init_user(..., memory_home.raw(), bootstrap.raw())`). AgentProcess holds `memory_home: caps::Handle` with the literal comment "Born-with ObjKind::MemoryHome (M13 fills the body)". So M13 is exactly "fill the body behind this handle" — NO new handle, manifest, or spawn-signature change.

2. caps::Object (caps.rs) is TODAY identity-only: `pub struct Object { pub kind: ObjKind }`, held behind `Rc<Object>` inside `Slot.object: Option<Rc<Object>>`. There is NO payload field yet — this is the seam M13 fills.

RECOMMENDED WIRING (zero-unsafe, keeps the M11 chokepoint as the only door): give Object an optional interior-mutable payload:
  `pub struct Object { pub kind: ObjKind, pub mem: Option<RefCell<crate::mem::MemSubstrate>> }`
(`use core::cell::RefCell;` — safe, no_std-OK). Keep current `mint()`/dup/narrow/transfer paths constructing `Object { kind, mem: None }`. Add `HandleTable::mint_memory_home(rights) -> Option<Handle>` that builds `Object { kind: MemoryHome, mem: Some(RefCell::new(MemSubstrate::new(backing))) }`. In `agent_spawn`, swap the born-with line to `table.mint_memory_home(mh_rights)`. Each agent thus gets its OWN MemSubstrate behind its OWN MemoryHome Object — this is structurally the `memory:private/<agent>` namespace (one substrate per born-with home; the manifest-granted second MemoryHome cap in MANIFEST_*_CAPS stays a plain identity object).

3. The Rc + RefCell pair is the right choice because dispatch only holds `&mut HandleTable` and reaches the object via shared `&Rc<Object>`; RefCell gives the `&mut MemSubstrate` for write/recall while the single-core, interrupts-masked discipline (same one AGENTS/ctx_switch rely on) guarantees no reentrant borrow. ALTERNATIVE rejected: a side registry keyed by `principal` passed into dispatch — it breaks "reached ONLY through the handle" and changes the dispatch signature. The Object-payload approach is preferred.
recon.tb_mem_abi:
POINTER-FREE ABI over `SyscallArgs{ method:u32, handle:Handle, args:[u64;4] }` -> `SysReturn{ status, value:u64 }` (ONE scalar out). Target handle is always the memory_home cap; values/keys are INLINE SCALARS or object handles at M13 (real text buffers arrive with M14 channels).

KEY DECISION — the WRITE vs WRITE_PROCEDURAL tension (must be resolved): the born-with home carries `WRITE`, but reserved `M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18` requires `WRITE_PROCEDURAL` (T4, the CoALA privileged-skill write, MEMORY-SPEC §1 T4 + SELF-IMPROVEMENT). An agent writing T2/T3 via method 18 would be DENIED. The task says "gated by WRITE". RECOMMENDATION: keep method 18/`WRITE_PROCEDURAL` reserved for the T4 procedural write at M18 (preserve the asymmetry), and add two bodies in the explicitly-reserved sparse band (caps.rs header invites "M13 adds method BODIES"):
  - `M_MEM_WRITE  = 23` requires `Rights::WRITE`  -> tb_mem_write (T2 episodic / T3 semantic ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP)
  - `M_MEM_READ   = 24` requires `Rights::READ`   -> tb_mem_read (direct read-your-writes by id)
  - reuse `M_MEM_RECALL = 19` requires `Rights::RECALL` -> tb_recall (activation-ranked)
  - reuse `M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE = 20` requires `Rights::CONSOLIDATE` -> tb_mem_manage/consolidate
Add the two numbers to `required_right()` and route 19/20/23/24 in `dispatch()`. (Alternative if "zero new numbers" is mandatory: repurpose required_right(18) to WRITE and split a fresh procedural-write number at M18 — but that muddies the documented T4 asymmetry; or add WRITE_PROCEDURAL to mh_rights — but that violates "no manifest change".)

ENCODINGS (args are [u64;4]):
- tb_mem_write (M_MEM_WRITE, WRITE): args[0]=op {0 ADD,1 UPDATE,2 DELETE->tombstone,3 NOOP} (Mem0 four-op vocab); args[1]=key/lexical-token id (interned small-int "word"/tag supplied by userspace; M13 self-test uses synthetic ids — NO embeddings needed); args[2]=value scalar (or a `Handle.raw()` to richer content later); args[3]=packed {importance 1..=10 in low byte (GA poignancy), tier + provenance flags in high bytes}. Returns `SysReturn::ok(record_id)`.
- tb_mem_read (M_MEM_READ, READ): args[0]=record_id. Returns `ok(value)` — INSTANT RYW over the append-only T2 journal.
- tb_recall (M_MEM_RECALL, RECALL): args[0]=query token id; args[1]=Finsts cursor / exclude_recent flag (0=fresh, else retrieve_next continuation — the return-same-result breaker); args[2]=k/max; args[3]=packed tier-mask + optional w_a/w_r/w_i weights (default all =1). Returns `ok(record_handle.raw())` — COPY-ON-RETRIEVE mints an `ObjKind::MemoryRecord` (already reserved) into the caller's table (Soar LTI->STI copy) and returns its handle; the agent then M_OBJECT_INSPECT/reads the scalar. Subsequent calls with the Finsts cursor advance past the just-returned set.
- tb_mem_manage / consolidate (M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE, CONSOLIDATE): args[0]=manage op {set t_invalid tombstone, add typed link, demote tier, pin into a T0 register}; args[1..]=operands. Returns `ok(count)`. (M13 ships a minimal SYNCHRONOUS consolidate to prove the gated path; the async kswapd-style daemon is M17.)

Because SysReturn carries a single u64, recall/read return an id-or-handle, never a buffer — fully pointer-free, M11-faithful, and forward-compatible with M14 buffer payloads.
recon.substrate_location: NEW SAFE MODULE: crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs, file-top inner attribute `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (matches caps.rs). Wire it in crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs right beside the caps line (~L41): add `mod mem;` (or `pub mod mem;` if the kernel self-test needs to name `tb_hal::mem::*` types directly). The crate is already `#![no_std]` with `extern crate alloc;` brought online crate-wide, so mem.rs uses `alloc::{vec::Vec, collections::BTreeMap, string::String}` freely — pure safe heap structures + indices, ZERO new unsafe (the M13 invariant). caps.rs gains `use crate::mem::MemSubstrate;` + `use core::cell::RefCell;` to hold the payload; both files stay forbid(unsafe). NOTE: lib.rs has `#![deny(missing_docs)]`, so every `pub` item in mem.rs needs a doc comment. No Cargo.toml change required for the RAM-backed default (BLA/BM25 done in fixed-point integer math to avoid a float/libm dep — see risks; if floats are wanted, add the no_std-safe `libm` crate, still zero unsafe).
recon.tier_datastructures:
All in crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs, no_std safe Rust (alloc). `pub struct MemSubstrate { t0, t1, t2, t3, finsts, clock:u64, quota, backing }` where `clock` is the logical transaction counter feeding bi-temporal `t_created`.

T0 CONTEXT REGISTERS (ACT-R buffers — bounded, NO unbounded blob): fixed array `registers: [Option<Register>; N_REG]` (e.g. N_REG=8: goal/retrieval/percept/tool-result/...). `struct Register { name_tok:u64, kind:RegKind, value:u64 }`. The prompt is materialized from these; const-bounded by construction.

T1 WORKING GRAPH (Soar WM — state-rooted, unreachable auto-GC): `struct WorkingGraph { nodes: Vec<WmNode>, root: u32 }`, `struct WmNode { id:u32, attr_tok:u64, val:u64, edges:Vec<u32>, support:Support }` with `enum Support { ISupport, OSupport }` (i-support = justifying thought, auto-retracted). GC = mark-and-sweep reachability from `root` (pure safe Vec walk).

T2 EPISODIC JOURNAL (Soar EpMem flight-recorder — append-only, bi-temporal, instant RYW): `struct EpisodicJournal { log: Vec<Episode>, next_id:u64 }`; `struct Episode { id:u64, content_tok:u64, value:u64, t_created:u64, t_expired:u64, t_valid:u64, t_invalid:u64, provenance:Provenance, producing_task:u32 }`. Append = `log.push(...)` then immediately readable -> read-your-writes INSTANT. This is the lossless canonical store; T1/T3 are indices/distillations over it.

T3 SEMANTIC STORE (distilled records + lexical index, activation-ranked — NO embeddings at M13): `struct SemanticStore { records: Vec<MemRecord>, lexical: BTreeMap<u64 /*token*/, Vec<u32 /*record idx*/>> }` (the BTreeMap is the inverted index = BM25 over interned token ids). `MemRecord` carries the MEMORY-SPEC §2 fields used by the additive score: `importance:u8 (1..=10)`, `access:{count:u32, last_k_ts:[u64;10]}` (O(1) BLA state, ACT-R/Petrov), `utility:{c_succ:u32, c_use:u32}`, `links:Vec<Link{kind:LinkKind(Cites|Relates|Supersedes), to:u32}>`, bi-temporal `t_*`, `provenance`. RANKING: `score = w_a*BLA(d=0.5) + w_r*relevance + w_i*importance`, components min-max normalized, default w=1 — BLA = ln(Σ tΔ^-0.5) over `last_k_ts` (do in fixed-point integer to stay float-free/Kani-friendly), relevance = BM25 token overlap from `lexical`, importance from the field. Three-stage retrieval: (1) candidate search via `lexical` (+ T2 scan), (2) RRF/MMR rerank, (3) templated context. Spreading-activation/partial-match/noise OFF by default (ACT-R conservatism).

FINSTS (the return-same-result breaker): `struct Finsts { recent: [Option<(u64 /*id*/, u64 /*ts*/)>; F] }` — bounded + time-limited "just returned" set; `exclude_recent`/`retrieve_next` skip members. COPY-ON-RETRIEVE: recall does not mutate the store; it mints an ObjKind::MemoryRecord working-copy handle (only access-metadata is written back on the read path, relatime-style).
recon.selftest_plan:
SLOT: kernel/src/main.rs, a new `{ ... }` block placed AFTER `tb_hal::serial_write_str("M12: agent OK\n");` and BEFORE the final `tb_hal::halt()`. Mirror the M11/M12 style: a local `fn m13_fail(why:&str)->!` that prints `"M13: FAIL "` + reason + halts (so the runner's grep fails loudly), ending with `tb_hal::serial_write_str("M13: memory OK\n");`. The timer was already `timer_disarm()`-ed before the M12 verdict, so M13 runs SINGLE-THREADED with interrupts masked — exactly the discipline the MemSubstrate RefCell relies on. NO userspace embeddings: all keys/queries are synthetic interned token ids chosen in the test; BM25/BLA operate on them.

Two complementary witnesses (M11/M12 precedent of kernel-side + through-the-agent):

A) SELF-CONTAINED kernel-side proof (primary, like the M11 algebra block): build a local `caps::HandleTable`, `mint_memory_home(READ|WRITE|RECALL|CONSOLIDATE)`, then drive `caps::dispatch`:
  1. WRITE: M_MEM_WRITE op=ADD, key=tokK, value=V0, importance=9 -> Ok(id0). Add 2-3 more records with varied tokens/importance/recency.
  2. READ-YOUR-WRITES: M_MEM_READ id0 -> Ok && value==V0 immediately after the write (proves instant RYW over append-only T2).
  3. RECALL (activation-ranked): M_MEM_RECALL query=tokK -> Ok(handle) resolving to the highest additive-score record (assert it is the high-importance+recent one). Recall again with the Finsts/exclude_recent cursor -> returns the NEXT candidate, not the same id (proves the return-same-result breaker). Verify the returned handle is an ObjKind::MemoryRecord minted into the table (copy-on-retrieve).
  4. CONSOLIDATE (gated success): M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE op=tombstone/merge -> Ok (this home HAS the CONSOLIDATE right); assert a record's t_invalid set or a dedup count returned.
  5. RIGHTS-DENIED PATH: re-mint a home with READ|RECALL only (no WRITE) -> M_MEM_WRITE -> Denied; and a home without CONSOLIDATE -> M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE -> Denied. (Both fall straight out of the existing required_right gate — no special-casing.)

B) THROUGH-THE-BORN-WITH-HOME proof (the kernel-guarantee witness): the existing manifests already line this up — Agent A's home = READ|WRITE|RECALL (write+recall OK, consolidate DENIED, no CONSOLIDATE), Agent B's home = READ|RECALL (write DENIED — clean denied path with zero new setup). Add a small tb-hal facade (AGENTS is private), e.g. `pub fn agent_mem_dispatch(task, method, a0,a1,a2,a3) -> Option<(u32,u64)>` that runs `caps::dispatch` against the agent's stored `memory_home` handle, so the test exercises write->RYW->recall on Agent A's ACTUAL born-with home and asserts Agent B's write is Denied — proving the substrate is a real per-agent guarantee, not a local toy.

VERDICT: all of {RYW match, recall picks the ranked record, Finsts advances, consolidate Ok, both denied paths Denied} must hold together before printing `M13: memory OK`.
recon.integration_points:
EXACT touch points (all additive, no ABI/manifest/handle break):
1. crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs — NEW file, `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`: MemSubstrate + T0/T1/T2/T3 structs, Finsts, BackingStore + RamStore, fixed-point BLA/BM25, and `MemSubstrate::{write,read,recall,manage,consolidate}` methods.
2. crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs ~L41 — add `mod mem;` (or `pub mod mem;`) beside `pub mod caps;`.
3. crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs — (a) `Object` gains `mem: Option<RefCell<crate::mem::MemSubstrate>>`; add `use core::cell::RefCell; use crate::mem;`; (b) `HandleTable::mint_memory_home(rights)`; (c) add `M_MEM_WRITE=23`/`M_MEM_READ=24` consts + entries in `required_right()` (WRITE/READ); (d) add 19/20/23/24 arms to `dispatch()` that resolve the object, match `ObjKind::MemoryHome` + `mem: Some(cell)`, `cell.borrow_mut()` and call the MemSubstrate op (clone the Rc out and drop the slot borrow BEFORE minting the MemoryRecord copy-on-retrieve so the table `&mut` doesn't alias). Stays forbid(unsafe).
4. crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs `agent_spawn` (~L1290) — swap the born-with `table.mint(ObjKind::MemoryHome, mh_rights)` for `table.mint_memory_home(mh_rights)`. Optionally add `pub fn agent_mem_dispatch(task, method, a0..a3)` facade for the through-the-agent self-test (AGENTS is private). `agent_syscall_current` already forwards args[a0,a1,0,0]+method via caps::dispatch — extend to pass a2/a3 (currently hardcoded 0,0) so the full [u64;4] write/recall encoding reaches dispatch.
5. kernel/src/main.rs — add the M13 self-test block after the "M12: agent OK" print, before `halt()`, ending with the `"M13: memory OK\n"` marker. Reuses existing MANIFEST_A (WRITE) / MANIFEST_B (no WRITE) for the permitted/denied split.
RIGHTS already reserved in caps.rs: Rights::WRITE(1<<1), RECALL(1<<8), CONSOLIDATE(1<<9), WRITE_PROCEDURAL(1<<7); ObjKind::MemoryHome + ObjKind::MemoryRecord already exist; methods M_MEM_RECALL=19, M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20, M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 already reserved.
risks: WRITE_PROCEDURAL vs WRITE mismatch (the central decision): reserved M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 requires WRITE_PROCEDURAL but the born-with home carries only WRITE, so a T2/T3 write via method 18 would be Denied. Resolve by adding M_MEM_WRITE=23(WRITE)+M_MEM_READ=24(READ) and keeping 18/WRITE_PROCEDURAL for the privileged T4 procedural write at M18 (preferred); do NOT add WRITE_PROCEDURAL to mh_rights (would violate 'no manifest change') and do NOT silently downgrade method 18's right (erases the CoALA T4 asymmetry).

'No ABI change' status note (ROADMAP L380) vs the need for write/read/manage+recall = 4 verbs over 3 reserved numbers. Reconcile per caps.rs's own header ('M13 adds method BODIES'): adding two numbers in the explicitly-reserved sparse band [16,..) grows the closed method set without breaking the M11 rights-mask invariant — but call this out so it is a deliberate, reviewed choice, not a surprise.

Object payload mutation soundness: Object is shared behind Rc, so mutation needs RefCell interior mutability. A reentrant borrow would panic. Safe ONLY because M13 dispatch runs single-core with interrupts masked (the same discipline AGENTS/ctx_switch assume). Copy-on-retrieve mints a MemoryRecord into the table (mutating it) WHILE reading the substrate — must clone the Rc<Object> out and drop the RefCell borrow + slot borrow before table.alloc to avoid an aliasing/borrow conflict.

no_std float for BLA(d=0.5)=ln(Σ tΔ^-0.5): core has no ln/powf. Either add the no_std-safe `libm` crate (still zero unsafe, one Cargo.toml dep) or implement BLA + min-max normalization in fixed-point integer math (recommended: zero deps, deterministic, Kani-friendly). Do NOT reach for std.
deny(missing_docs) is crate-wide: every pub item in mem.rs (and the new caps.rs consts/fn) needs a doc comment or the build fails.

Heap pressure / bounds: T2 is append-only Vec and T3 carries a BTreeMap inverted index; without the token write-amplification Quota (KeyKOS space-bank) a runaway writer can exhaust the M5/M7 heap. Bound T0 (const N_REG), Finsts (const F), and meter T2/T3 growth via Quota; the HandleTable cap is 16 so copy-on-retrieve MemoryRecord mints can hit ObjFull — return the closed status, never panic.

Durability scope is explicitly RAM-only at M13 (BackingStore = RamStore); a crash loses memory. This is the accepted ROADMAP decision (durable virtio-blk deferred) but the BackingStore trait + epoch() freshness marker must be in place now so M16/future durable backing slots in without touching tier logic.

agent_syscall_current currently passes args:[a0,a1,0,0] — the full [u64;4] write/recall encoding (op,key,value,importance / query,cursor,k,weights) needs a2/a3 plumbed through, else high args are silently zeroed.
